agent_notes: Waltham Forest produces a quality longlist but not 30-35 PAINTS-safe venues; the limiting factors are Books & Coffee scarcity, William Morris/Vestry closure works, and strict Creative Walk park rules.
Creative Walks are limited to Walthamstow Wetlands because it is the only Waltham Forest route found on the PAINTS v6 approved safety list; Lloyd Park and Epping Forest were not used as walks.
Google ratings/review counts, exact Google walking times, bank-holiday hours, private-hire conflicts, and 8-12 walk-in tolerance still need host confirmation before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Official site/search result lists shop and drink Tue-Thu 14:00-23:00, Fri-Sat 12:00-23:00, Sun 12:00-18:00; dining hours are narrower. Bank-holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookings are available; normal walk-in tolerance for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Use an early Tue-Thu slot or PAINTS-held table.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good candidate for 8-10 if pre-called; 12 likely needs a held table because it is a village wine bar/restaurant.
venues.capacity_notes: Small independent shop/bar/restaurant with tasting programme and short food menu; avoid peak Friday/Saturday.
venues.why_fits_paints: A polished but local wine-shop/bar on Orford Road, with enough bottle labels, food residencies, and village texture to give strangers things to point at and talk about. The Anglo-Sicilian/short-menu signal gives it more depth than a generic wine bar. It is one of the clearest Waltham Forest Show & Tell anchors.
venues.anecdote_story: The Times 2026 Walthamstow area write-up singles out Hometipple as a convivial wine bar with an Anglo-Sicilian chef; the venue also runs public tastings via Eventbrite.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine shop and bar, low-intervention/natural-leaning wine, dinner plates, tastings.
venues.safety_notes: Orford Road is a high-footfall village street; route attendees via Walthamstow Central or Walthamstow Queen’s Road after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min walk via Forest Road - host should verify exact Google walking route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small size and unverified 8-12 walk-in capacity; a host-held booking may be needed.

venues.opening_hours: Official contact page lists Mon-Wed 15:00-20:00, Thu-Fri 12:00-21:00, Sat 12:00-21:00, Sun 12:00-18:00. Bank-holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Wine shop/bar format; casual drinks are implied, but 8-12 group walk-in tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Plausible for an early tasting-style gathering, but the room is compact and needs a call ahead.
venues.capacity_notes: Natural/organic wine shop with bar environment, deli snacks and tastings.
venues.why_fits_paints: Forest Wines has the right PAINTS mechanics: shelves, producer stories, orange wine/pet-nat curiosity, and a long-running local identity. It is less restaurant-like than Hometipple, which can be an advantage for show-and-tell label browsing. Strong backup pairing with nearby Gnarly Vines/Hometipple.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue says it has been a Walthamstow community hub for more than 14 years and specialises in natural, organic, biodynamic and vegan wines.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Natural and organic wines, orange wine, pet-nat, craft beer, deli snacks and tastings.
venues.safety_notes: Forest Road near Blackhorse Road is well connected; finish before late-night quiet industrial stretches if attendees disperse alone.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min walk - host should verify exact Google walking route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Official walk-in/group policy is not explicit online.

venues.opening_hours: The Nudge lists Mon 12:00-18:00, Sun and Tue-Thu 12:00-20:00, Fri-Sat from 11:00 with later bar hours; exact current official hours and bank holidays UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Shop/bar format suggests walk-ins, but 8-12 group walk-in tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Better for 8 than 12 unless the host arranges a quiet early slot.
venues.capacity_notes: Natural wine shop and bar; compact Bakers Arms unit.
venues.why_fits_paints: A neighbourhood natural-wine shop/bar with a playful producer-led range, it gives a PAINTS host lots of bottles and origin stories to work with. It is slightly less seated-dinner friendly than Hometipple but stronger than a normal pub for creative conversation. Good Bakers Arms counterweight to the Walthamstow Village cluster.
venues.anecdote_story: Public copy frames the venue around natural, organic and biodynamic producers, orange wine, pet-nat and low-intervention bottles.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Natural wine, orange wine, pet-nats, beer and shop browsing.
venues.safety_notes: Hoe Street/Bakers Arms has bus and high-street footfall; route attendees to Walthamstow Central or bus corridors after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min walk - host should verify exact Google walking route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Online hours/policy are thinner than Forest Wines; host confirmation essential.

venues.venue_name: Renegade Urban Winery / LEO at Renegade
venues.opening_hours: Official page lists Thu 17:00-23:00, Fri 12:00-23:00, Sat 12:00-23:00, Sun 12:00-18:00. Bank-holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookings are promoted; group walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Treat as book/hold table to preserve PAINTS reliability.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong if booked/held: winery kitchen and bar format supports a proper 2-hour group.
venues.capacity_notes: Modern bistro inside an urban winery; larger and more operationally resilient than micro wine shops.
venues.why_fits_paints: A working urban winery is a built-in show-and-tell machine: tanks, bottles, London-made wine and a food pairing angle. It is more expensive and more known than the tiny bars, but the story and setting are unusually strong for Waltham Forest. Best used when PAINTS wants a premium drinks event that still feels local.
venues.anecdote_story: Renegade positions the site as an urban winery kitchen and bar where wines are made on-site and food is designed around the pours.
venues.serve_or_on_view: London-made wine, bistro food, winery setting and pairing suggestions.
venues.safety_notes: Lockwood Way is an industrial/leisure pocket near Blackhorse Road; route attendees together to Blackhorse Road station after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 3-5 min walk within Blackhorse Lane/Lockwood Way area.
venues.risks_watchouts: Booking dependence and price point; demote if walk-ins cannot be supported.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon-Wed 12:00-23:00, Thu 12:00-23:00, Fri-Sat 12:00-01:00, Sun 12:00-20:00 with kitchen hours. Bank-holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official FAQ says walk-ins are accepted whenever possible and seats are held back; booking strongly recommended Thu-Sat.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Operationally strong for 8-12, but less intimate; choose a quieter corner and avoid major event nights.
venues.capacity_notes: Very large beer hall/social venue with food, bars and events.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is not a delicate PAINTS jewel, but it is a practical Blackhorse backup with scale, walk-ins and local industrial energy. It works when a group needs reliability over intimacy. Keep it Tier B because the social-hall format can flatten the show-and-tell mood.
venues.anecdote_story: Big Penny Social describes itself as a new type of community venue; it sits in the Blackhorse Lane creative/leisure cluster.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Beer, wine, cocktails, kitchen, quizzes, music and events.
venues.safety_notes: Use Blackhorse Road station route; late Friday/Saturday crowds and 1am close make early slots safer.
venues.backup_venue: Renegade Urban Winery / LEO at Renegade
venues.risks_watchouts: Event programming can create noise; check diary and avoid party nights.

venues.opening_hours: Pillars official page says open Tue-Sun; CAMRA lists Tue-Thu 16:00-22:30, Fri-Sat 12:00-22:30, Sun 12:00-21:30. Bank holidays UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Instagram/search snippets mention booking a table and weekend activity; 8-12 walk-in tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely workable at off-peak times in a pub/taproom setting; call ahead for a table cluster.
venues.capacity_notes: Craft beer pub in CRATE St James Street with roof terrace/food/events signals.
venues.why_fits_paints: Pillars brings a local brewery story and a less obvious St James Street setting. It is beer-first rather than wine-first, so it just misses Tier A for Show & Tell Drinks, but the independent pub texture is useful. Strong backup to the Village/Blackhorse concentration.
venues.anecdote_story: Pillars presents itself as London’s first independent lager brewery; the pub is in the council-owned CRATE St James Street development.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Independent beer taps, craft beer, street food/events.
venues.safety_notes: St James Street has Overground access; route attendees via St James Street station after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min walk - host should verify exact Google walking route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Beer-led not wine-led; event noise can interfere with conversation.

venues.opening_hours: Council page and market Instagram/site list Saturday 10:00-16:00 with around 30 food stalls. Weather/cancellation handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm close to date.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; no booking. Meeting-point cafe/walk-in capacity for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for a hosted browse if the group meets just outside stall pinch-points.
venues.capacity_notes: Around 30 food stalls; producer and local/social-enterprise trader commitment.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is Waltham Forest’s strongest market pick: a weekly food market in the borough’s flagship cultural park, right by William Morris Gallery. It has real producer energy, good visual texture and enough food variety for a roaming group. Pair with the gallery/park story rather than treating it as a generic shop.
venues.anecdote_story: The council says Lloyd Park Market welcomes local and social-enterprise traders; the market launched in 2019 with high-quality food stalls inside Lloyd Park.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Seasonal produce, organic meat, fresh fish, bakery, hot/cold cuisine and local traders.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Lloyd Park/Forest Road setting; Lloyd Park is not on the PAINTS Creative Walk list, so use only as market location/gallery setting, not a Creative Walk route.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 12-15 min walk/transit; market backup radius allows 15 min walk or transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Outdoor weather exposure and not an approved Creative Walk park.

venues.opening_hours: London Farmers Markets lists every Sunday 10:00-14:00, open weekly rain or shine, Town Square by 17 & Central. Host must confirm any holiday cancellation.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access farmers market; no booking. Nearby independent coffee meeting-point capacity for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a two-hour hosted browse; use a clear meeting point because Town Square can be busy.
venues.capacity_notes: Over 20 fresh farmyard stalls per council page; fresh produce and prepared food.
venues.why_fits_paints: A reliable farmers market with enough food-and-producer material for conversation, and a central location that keeps arrivals easy. It is less picturesque than Lloyd Park but stronger operationally. The market works best when framed as a Sunday provisions walk through Walthamstow’s civic centre.
venues.anecdote_story: Council/local-market copy links it to the top end of Walthamstow High Street and the borough’s regular market ecology.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fresh farm produce, bread, dairy, meats, prepared food and seasonal stalls.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime town-centre location by Victoria line/Overground; avoid routing through 17 & Central as a PAINTS venue because malls are excluded.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 5 min walk along High Street.
venues.risks_watchouts: Town-square/mall adjacency makes it less aesthetically special than Lloyd Park.

venues.opening_hours: Council event page lists Sunday Social Market every Sunday 10:00-16:00 at 116 High Street; market website also lists 10:00-16:00. Host must confirm live date.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for a casual hosted browse, but stronger as backup than primary because stall curation is broader.
venues.capacity_notes: Street-food/community/local-brand market on the High Street.
venues.why_fits_paints: Sunday Social adds food, local brands and high-street energy, but it is more general-purpose than Lloyd Park or the farmers market. It is useful as a weather/date backup and for groups who want bustle rather than pastoral charm. Keep Tier B to avoid overclaiming the curation.
venues.anecdote_story: Council newsletters say Sunday Social launched in 2018/2019 to platform independent and emerging street-food vendors.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street food, community entertainment and local brands.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime High Street route with strong footfall; watch crowd density and pick a calm meeting point.
venues.risks_watchouts: Website contains template artefacts and public timings vary on social channels; confirm operator/date.

venues.opening_hours: Council page describes Wood Street Indoor Market as operating since 1955; live stall/opening hours are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm before scheduling.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access indoor market when open; no booking. Stall availability varies.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a slow browse if enough stalls are open; host should scout beforehand.
venues.capacity_notes: Indoor market with vintage/collectables/independent stalls; exact active stall count unverified.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday late morning if confirmed open and staffed.
venues.why_fits_paints: It has genuine treasure-hunt character and local history, which PAINTS likes, but operational certainty is weaker than the weekly markets. The indoor format helps with weather. Use it only after a fresh stall-open check.
venues.anecdote_story: Waltham Forest describes it as a quirky local market open since 1955 and a Wood Street trove of surprises.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Vintage, records, collectables, independent traders, small food/cafe elements depending on day.
venues.safety_notes: Wood Street station/high-street arrival is simple; keep daytime.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15 min by bus/walk combination; market backup radius allows transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Variable stall openings and thinner current web data.

venues.venue_name: Creative Makers Market Walthamstow by The Creative Side of London
venues.opening_hours: Pedddle and organiser pages list 2026 dates including 18 July, 7 Nov and 5 Dec at Waltham Forest Community Hub; exact event hours/date roster UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access/popup market model; ticketing/entry requirements UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a small group if the room is not crowded; route as a hosted browse plus nearby coffee/drinks.
venues.capacity_notes: Handmade homeware, fashion, accessories, art and local designers.
venues.best_time_slot: On confirmed market dates, first hour after opening.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is more explicitly maker-led than the food markets, with craft, art and fashion objects that naturally prompt show-and-tell. Its limitation is cadence: it is not weekly and current date-specific operating detail must be checked. Keep Tier B despite good aesthetic fit.
venues.anecdote_story: The Creative Side of London says it hosts craft fairs across Leyton, Leytonstone, Walthamstow and Chingford and lists Waltham Forest 2026 dates.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Handcrafted homeware, fashion, accessories, art and small-maker stalls.
venues.safety_notes: Walthamstow Village daytime/early evening is a comfortable arrival zone; use Walthamstow Central route.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15 min by bus/walk combination on a Saturday; market backup radius allows transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Cadence and exact hours are the main risk.

venues.venue_name: Walthamstow Wetlands Engine House to Coppermill Route
venues.opening_hours: London Wildlife Trust/Discover Waltham Forest list April-September 09:30-17:00, October-March 09:30-16:00; Engine House Cafe summer 09:30-16:30. Bank-holiday/special closures UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access reserve; no venue booking. Engine House Cafe group walk-in capacity is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a daytime 8-12 walk with clear pacing and no dogs except assistance dogs.
venues.capacity_notes: Large 211-hectare wetland reserve with visitor centre, cafe, toilets, bird hides and accessible routes.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the only Waltham Forest Creative Walk anchor that appears on the PAINTS v6 safety list, and it is strong: reservoirs, bird hides, Victorian water infrastructure and big-sky views. Suggested stops: Engine House, reservoir causeway, bird hide, Coppermill Tower view. It gives a quiet, curious 2-3 hour event without forcing unsafe or unapproved park routes.
venues.anecdote_story: Walthamstow Wetlands is described by the council as Europe’s largest urban wetland reserve, opened to the public in 2017, with a Victorian Engine House and historic Coppermill context.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Reservoir landscapes, birdlife, Engine House visitor centre/cafe, industrial waterworks heritage.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS-approved Safety-First Shortlist park/route: Walthamstow Wetlands. Daytime only; stick to official paths, observe closing times and assistance-dogs-only rule.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather exposure, seasonal closing times, assistance-dogs-only policy, and no other approved Waltham Forest park walk to substitute.

venues.opening_hours: Official home page says Tue-Sun 10:00-17:00, but Renewal Project 2026 means gallery spaces are partially closed from 22 June and full closures follow in summer; host must check exact date before publishing.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free entry/open to all normally; timed-entry not indicated. Group visit during renewal works is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good after reopening/when galleries are open; during renewal works, group route may be constrained.
venues.capacity_notes: Public gallery in Morris’s childhood Georgian villa; cafe/shop availability changes during renewal.
venues.best_time_slot: Wednesday-Friday 10:30-12:30 outside closure windows, or after phased reopening.
venues.why_fits_paints: The collection is almost too perfect for PAINTS: textiles, pattern, politics, craft and a house story all in one. It is Tier B now only because the July 2026 renewal programme makes operations fragile. After full reopening and a current exhibition check, it could move back to Tier A.
venues.anecdote_story: It is the only public museum devoted to William Morris, housed in the Georgian villa where he lived as a child, with the world’s most significant/largest collection of his work.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Morris textiles, design, craft, socialism/conservation story; current 2026 gallery access limited by renewal works and Earthly Paradise coming in October.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Lloyd Park/Forest Road is suitable; do not convert Lloyd Park into a Creative Walk because it is not on the PAINTS approved parks list.
venues.backup_venue: Winns Gallery / Lloyd Park pop-up exhibitions if open; otherwise no qualifying same-category backup within 10 min during closure.
venues.backup_walk_time: 2-5 min if Winns Gallery has an active exhibition; otherwise no qualifying backup found within 10 min.
venues.risks_watchouts: Biggest caveat in the district: partial/full closures in summer 2026 and lack of current exhibition access.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Ravenswood / Walthamstow Central or Wood Street
venues.opening_hours: Official site and Time Out list Fri-Sat 11:00-22:00, Sun 11:00-18:00; Rolling Scones cafe/bar open for food and drinks all weekend. Bank holidays/private shoots UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free/open visitor attraction during public hours; group of 8-12 tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm, especially for cafe seating.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good as a visual browse; cafe seating for a full 2-hour discussion must be confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Industrial-estate neon collection with cafe/bar; can be busy at weekends.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is maximal, strange and instantly talkable: neon art, movie props and signs inside an industrial estate. Unlike a normal gallery, the objects are pop-cultural and tactile enough for non-art people to engage. It earns Tier A despite obviousness because the setting and story are uniquely Walthamstow.
venues.anecdote_story: Visit London says it is the largest collection of neon lights in Europe, based on the personal collection of late neon artist Chris Bracey and including his works, discarded signs and movie props.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Neon signs, artworks, movie props, Rolling Scones cafe/bar.
venues.safety_notes: Use daytime or early evening; Ravenswood Industrial Estate is destination-heavy when open but quieter on surrounding residential routes after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 20 min transit/walk, so not within 10 min; closest same-category backup is not PAINTS-radius compliant.
venues.risks_watchouts: No same-category backup within 10 min; demoted to Tier B under PAINTS backup rule despite exceptional uniqueness and public access.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Sat 10:00-16:00, Sun 12:00-16:00. Current show pages should be checked week-of because exhibitions rotate.
venues.walk_in_policy: Small independent gallery with public weekend opening; 8-12 group walk-in tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Works for 8-10 as a short gallery visit followed by coffee/pub discussion; 12 may crowd the room.
venues.capacity_notes: Small not-for-profit gallery; use as part of a hybrid Leytonstone cultural slot.
venues.why_fits_paints: A small independent not-for-profit gallery gives Leytonstone a genuine visual-art anchor without the obviousness of WMG or God’s Own Junkyard. It is intimate, local and adaptable to rotating shows. The small room means host control matters.
venues.anecdote_story: The gallery presents itself as an independent not-for-profit space in Leytonstone; The London Group lists window exhibitions there, showing connection to working artists.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Rotating contemporary art/window exhibitions; current programme must be checked.
venues.safety_notes: Church Lane is central Leytonstone near tube/bus routes; good daytime access.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 20 min transit/walk, outside strict 10 min; no qualifying same-category backup found within 10 min.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small scale, short weekend hours, and no same-category backup within 10 min; Tier B under PAINTS backup rule.

venues.opening_hours: Recent ArtRabbit/Instagram listings show Lloyd Park Studio Artists at Winns Gallery during E17 Art Trail 13 June 2026 and other June 2026 pop-ups; regular public hours are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm active exhibition.
venues.walk_in_policy: Public pop-up exhibitions when active; 8-12 group walk-in tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable only when an exhibition is active and staffed.
venues.capacity_notes: Small gallery/event space in Lloyd Park/Aveling Centre.
venues.why_fits_paints: When open, it is a lovely local-artist counterpoint to William Morris Gallery and Lloyd Park Market. It cannot be a primary PAINTS anchor without a live exhibition date. Include as Tier B because it is useful as a micro-gallery backup during active programmes.
venues.anecdote_story: Waltham Forest Echo describes Winns Gallery as nestled behind the park cafe and contributing to a summer of culture; ArtRabbit lists Lloyd Park Studio Artists exhibitions there.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Local artist pop-up exhibitions, design/photography/fashion depending on hire.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Lloyd Park only; not a Creative Walk route under PAINTS rules.
venues.backup_walk_time: 2-5 min walk if WMG galleries are open.
venues.risks_watchouts: Active exhibition and open hours are uncertain.

venues.opening_hours: Official site/contact page lists Mon closed, Tue-Sat 10:00-18:00, Sun 10:00-17:00, with weekday lunch closure 13:00-14:00. Bank holidays UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop/cafe open to public; 8-12 seated group walk-in tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best for 8-10 at a quiet morning/early afternoon; 12 for two hours may need explicit permission.
venues.capacity_notes: Independent bookshop with integrated bar/cafe; small Francis Road room.
venues.why_fits_paints: Phlox is the district’s cleanest Books & Coffee fit because the books and coffee are genuinely integrated rather than adjacent by convenience. The Francis Road setting adds a calm village-street arc for arrivals. It is strong but lonely: I did not find another Waltham Forest bookshop-cafe of comparable quality for a close backup.
venues.anecdote_story: Leytonstoner interviews connect the name Phlox to founder Aimée’s parents’ Belfast lifestyle concept store/cafe/design studio, revived in Leyton as books, booze and coffee.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Books, Allpress coffee, craft beers, natural wines and events/readings.
venues.safety_notes: Francis Road is a calm pedestrian-friendly local high street; use daytime or early evening.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying bookshop-cafe backup found within 10 min - closest cultural backup is Gravity Well Taproom or local cafes, but they do not meet Books & Coffee rules.
venues.risks_watchouts: No qualifying Books & Coffee backup within 10 min; demoted to Tier B under PAINTS backup rule despite excellent category fit.

venues.opening_hours: Official Leyton taproom page lists Mon-Thu 16:00-22:00, Fri 16:00-23:00, Sat 12:00-23:00, Sun 13:00-21:00. Bank holidays UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Taproom/public bar; 8-12 walk-in tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm, especially at peak.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely workable at off-peak arch/taproom tables; pre-call for a settled group.
venues.capacity_notes: Railway-arch brewery taproom with local food partner signals and guest beers.
venues.why_fits_paints: Gravity Well has a strong visual/producer story for beer lovers: railway arch, cosmic branding, and fresh small-batch beers. It is not the strictest drinks-category fit because PAINTS prioritises wine bars/character pubs, so Tier B is fair. Useful as a Leyton backup near Phlox/William IV.
venues.anecdote_story: The brewery started as a nano-brewery in a railway arch, with public material centring hazy, juicy pale ales and IPAs.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Own-brewed craft beer, guest beers, cans, food partner.
venues.safety_notes: Directly by Leyton Midland Road Overground; good station proximity after dark.
venues.risks_watchouts: Beer/taproom category fit is weaker than wine bars; can be noisy.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon 15:00-22:00, Tue-Thu 15:00-23:00, Fri-Sat 12:00-00:00, Sun 12:00-22:00; kitchen hours also published. Bank holidays UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub format; official booking/group walk-in tolerance for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: More group-resilient than micro taprooms; call ahead for seating.
venues.capacity_notes: Traditional East London pub under Exale stewardship with private events room and beer garden.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is a practical, characterful pub rather than a rare discovery. The Exale/Walthamstow brewing link and reborn old-boozer setting give enough story to work as a Show & Tell backup. Tier B because sports/private events and pub noise can overwhelm the mood.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue describes itself as an East End original, rebuilt under Walthamstow’s Exale Brewing, pouring tank-fresh beer and curated wine.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Tank-fresh beer, cask ale, cocktails, curated wine, Short Road pizza.
venues.safety_notes: High Road Leyton with bus/Overground access; avoid late sports-night dispersal.
venues.risks_watchouts: Sports/events may conflict with quiet conversation; independence is brewery-pub rather than owner-run wine bar.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Tempting local-history museum, but official pages say galleries and garden are temporarily closed for revitalisation and scheduled to reopen in 2026/autumn 2026; cannot pass Gate 1 on 2026-07-04.
Older/local listings conflict, but London Farmers Markets currently marks Leytonstone Farmers Market as no longer open; fails live operational gate.
Historic and huge, but too general-purpose/high-street retail for PAINTS Market Days compared with farmer/maker markets; no clear curated producer/craft identity above score floor.
Chain bookshop; PAINTS v6 rejects chains and Books & Coffee requires independent coffee/book identity.
Independent-bookshop lead found, but no verifiable on-site or literally-next-door independent coffee offer with 2-hour seating/group walk-in support; below Books & Coffee gate.
Lloyd Park is useful for markets/gallery adjacency but is not on the PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk parks list; cannot be sourced as a Creative Walk.
Epping Forest is explicitly listed as removed/do-not-source in PAINTS v6 safety guidance, so it cannot be used as a Creative Walk or backup.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Lee Valley / extended Lee Navigation routes
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: PAINTS v6 explicitly prohibits Lee Valley Regional Park and extended Lee Navigation routes for Creative Walks; Walthamstow Wetlands is the only approved local walk anchor included.
